corroboration: ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts — createChedPPNotificationUpToTransportDetails + createChedPPNotification are the authoritative statement of intended order
~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts — names every page on the happy path

urlBase.note: The /upload/... attach pages are served by a third app, on the same host as whichever app linked to them.

source.urlNormalisation: pageGuess URLs in the journey JSONs are LLM-extracted and inconsistent: some drop the /notifications/:draftId/ prefix, some substitute a /consignment/ segment for the real one. urlPattern below is the MAJORITY form across all 59 traces, cross-checked against the three fully-qualified traces (219b882e, e10b7cd5, 1f5b6eef). Where the majority disagreed with a single trace, the majority won.

primaryEvidenceTraces.219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149: B2C Article 72 create->submit. Fully-qualified URLs, cleanest core journey. Preferred evidence from transport onwards. CAVEAT: its URL labels lag by one page across the commodity region (it puts 'Internal market' on page-4 when the workflow + e10b7cd5 put it on page-5), so commodity-region evidence comes from e10b7cd5 instead.
primaryEvidenceTraces.e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572: HMI auto-completion. Clean URLs and the ONLY trace covering variety-of-genus-and-species + commodity/summary. Preferred evidence for the commodity region.
primaryEvidenceTraces.1f5b6eef3d110815e713ed79998cec2cb53026ee: 63 pages: create->submit->inspect->amend->resubmit. Fullest trace; preferred evidence for the decision app.
primaryEvidenceTraces.9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada: CSV upload + DoA agent org selection. ONLY trace covering consignment-for / consignment-organisation / csv-upload.
primaryEvidenceTraces.a61cf55bc26138194f50d589181543a2714db2ec: CUC billing. ONLY trace covering the four billing pages.
primaryEvidenceTraces.ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667: Cloning entry pages (all three cloning traces fail at the summary — see notes).

keyDedupRulings: commodity-class and variety-of-genus-and-species are ONE page, not two. Trace e10b7cd5 action 26 fires getByLabel('Select class') while the page URL is /commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species?commodity-selected-code=08059000. CommodityClassPage.ts is a page object over a control that lives on the variety page — it is not a page of its own.
The 'Notification Hub' (/overview), the amend-mode landing (/overview?source=dashboard) and the copy-as-new landing are the SAME page in different states — merged into notification-hub.
The post-split review with Valid/Rejected tabs is the SAME URL and heading as review-notification — merged, with the tabs recorded as a conditional state rather than a separate page.

EconomicOperatorsOverview / SelectEconomicOperator / AddEconomicOperator / ConfirmEconomicOperator (used by the cloning workflow) are the same four pages as traders-addresses / consignor-search / consignor-create / consignor-confirmation under a different label. Merged; aliases noted per page.

csv-upload and the 'Check uploaded commodity details' state are recorded as ONE page: CsvUploadPage.ts exposes btnChooseFile, btnUpload, the success banner and btnConfirmAndContinue as one page object, and neither trace block carries a URL. Evidence deliberately includes BOTH action 32 and action 34 so the snapshot wave settles one page vs two.

pages.name: Your import notifications (dashboard)
pages.conditional: always — journey entry point. Also the entry for search, copy-as-new, clone, amend and delete.

evidence.note: navigate to dashboard; action 8 is the 'Create a new notification' click that leaves the page

pages.name: What are you importing? (certificate type)
pages.conditional: always — CHED-PP is the 'Plants, plant products and other objects' radio.

pages.name: Who are you creating this notification for?
pages.conditional: DoA agent only — shown when the signed-in user may act on behalf of another organisation. Options: own organisation, or 'A different organisation'.

pages.name: Which company is this notification for?
pages.conditional: DoA agent only, and only after choosing 'A different organisation' on consignment-for. Selecting the agent's own org skips this page. Sets the orgId carried on the next page's query string.

pages.name: Origin of the plants, plant product or other objects (country of origin)
pages.conditional: always — single 'Country of origin' select. EU vs ROW origin changes downstream behaviour but not the page set.

pages.name: Origin of the import / add a reference number for this consignment
pages.conditional: always — the page where the DRAFT.GB.YYYY.NNNNNNN id first appears in the URL. Carries an optional consignment reference plus (per OriginOfImportPage.ts) 'Does this consignment conform...', 'Does your consignment require a health certificate?' and 'Will the consignment change after BCP...'. Every CHED-PP trace accepts the defaults and clicks straight through, so those three questions are NEVER exercised — see notes.

evidence.note: the page's only action in every core trace; the 'before' snapshot shows the rendered page

pages.name: How do you want to add your commodity details?
pages.conditional: always — the branch point: 'Manual entry' vs 'Upload from a CSV file'.

evidence.note: check radio 'Upload from a CSV file' — the other branch

pages.name: Upload commodity details CSV (and check uploaded details)
pages.conditional: CSV branch only — when 'Upload from a CSV file' was chosen on commodity-input-method. Replaces commodity-search / commodity-basic-description / commodity-bulk-details entirely: the CSV path runs csv-upload -> about-the-consignment -> notification-hub -> 'Additional details' -> commodity-additional-details.

evidence.note: click #button-upload; action 33 asserts the 'Upload successful' banner in #upload-banner
click 'Confirm and continue' — the LLM attributed this to a separate 'Check uploaded commodity details' page. Snapshot BOTH 32 and 34 to settle one page vs two.

pages.name: Search commodities (commodity code search)
pages.conditional: manual-entry branch only. Revisited once per additional commodity (reached from commodity-bulk-details 'Add commodity'). The bare /consignment/page-3 URL is the tab-chooser state before the 'Commodity code search' link is clicked.

evidence.note: second commodity 06042090 — proves the page is re-entered per commodity

pages.name: Commodity basic description / select species of commodity
pages.conditional: manual-entry branch only, once per commodity. Carries an optional EPPO-code species search ('EPPO code' textbox + Search + Add) — exercised only when the commodity requires a species. For simple commodities (e.g. 06011010 hyacinths) the page is a single Add + Save and continue. NOTE: traces 1f5b6eef/219b882e attribute the 'Add' click to the page-3 URL while e10b7cd5 and the workflow's own comments put btnAddSpecies on this page; the page-3/page-4 boundary is the one thing the snapshot wave should confirm.

evidence.note: click 'Add' species — unambiguously inside this trace's page-4 block (22-25)

pages.name: Variety of genus (and species) — includes 'Select class'
pages.conditional: Only for commodities whose species requires variety/class detail (e.g. 08059000 citrus with EPPO CIDAC). Skipped for the default 06011010/06042090 commodities, so ONLY trace e10b7cd5 covers it. This page also hosts the 'Select class' dropdown that CommodityClassPage.ts models — commodity-class is NOT a separate page.

evidence.note: select 'Select class' = 'Extra Class' while the page URL is /commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species — the proof that class and variety share one page

pages.name: Commodity (summary)
pages.conditional: Appears on the same species/variety path as variety-of-genus-and-species. ONLY trace e10b7cd5 covers it, and only as a single 'Save and continue' — the page's actual content is unverified.

evidence.note: the page's only action; snapshot the 'before' state

pages.name: Purpose of the consignment (about the consignment)
pages.conditional: always — 'Internal market' radio. Reached on BOTH the manual and CSV branches. 219b882e mislabels this page as page-4; e10b7cd5, 1f5b6eef and the CSV trace all agree it is page-5.

evidence.note: CSV branch reaches the same page

pages.name: Notification overview (task list hub)
pages.conditional: always — the task-list hub. Spokes per NotificationHubPage.ts: 'Commodity', #commodity-details-link (non-bulk), 'Additional details', 'Goods movement services', 'Transport to the port of entry'. Same page as the amend-mode landing (/notifications/:chedId/overview?source=dashboard) and the copy-as-new landing.

evidence.note: click 'Additional details' spoke (CSV branch) — a different spoke off the same hub

pages.name: Commodity details — bulk entry
pages.conditional: manual-entry branch only (the CSV branch skips it). Bulk-applies packages / type of package / quantity / quantity type / net weight / controlled atmosphere container / intended use / test-and-trial to all selected commodities via 'Select all' + 'Apply'. Also hosts the 'Add commodity' button that loops back to commodity-search.

evidence.note: select 'Select quantity type' = Bulbs — middle of the 32-40 range

pages.name: Consignment details — total gross weight
pages.conditional: always — reached on both branches. A single 'Total gross weight (kg)' textbox in every trace.

pages.name: Goods movement services declaration
pages.conditional: Only when gmsDeclaration = 'Yes'. NO CHED-PP SPEC SETS THIS, so zero traces cover it. Known only from ched-pp-workflows.ts (pages.gmsDeclaration.radioConfirmAboveStatement + btnSaveAndContinue) and GMSDeclarationPage.ts. Uncovered surface — a finding, not an absence of requirement.

pages.name: Transport before the BIP (point of entry, inspection premises, transport, arrival)
pages.conditional: always — the densest page in the journey: 'Entry border control post', 'Inspection premises', 'Choose from:' (transport method), 'Transport identification', 'Transport document reference', arrival Day/Month/Year + Hour/Minutes.

evidence.note: fill 'Transport document reference' — middle of the 43-53 range

pages.name: Goods movement services
pages.conditional: always — two radio groups: 'Are you using the Common Transit Convention...' and 'Will the transport use the...' service.

evidence.note: check 'Will the transport use the' = No

pages.name: Contact details (responsible person)
pages.conditional: always — pre-populated from the signed-in user; every trace clicks straight through, so the page's field surface is unverified.

pages.name: Nominated contacts
pages.conditional: always — every trace clicks straight through; field surface unverified.

pages.name: Accompanying documents
pages.conditional: always — 'Document type', 'Document reference', issue-date Day/Month/Year, then 'Add attachment' hands off to document-upload and the user returns here.

evidence.note: fill 'Document reference' = REF-124 — middle of the 51-56 range

pages.name: Upload attachment (upload service)
pages.conditional: Only when 'Add attachment' is clicked on accompanying-documents. A SEPARATE app (/upload/...), not the notification app — the strongest architectural signal in the corpus. Error states (file too large, empty file, disallowed type) render here: see traces 55fcf6fd (LARGE invoice) and bff12637 (empty file).

evidence.note: set input files example.png; action 58 is the 'Next' that leaves

pages.name: Addresses / traders (consignor, consignee, place of destination)
pages.conditional: always — a mini-hub. Links: 'Add a consignor or exporter', 'Add a consignee'; buttons: 'Same as consignee', #populate-importer, #populate-place-of-destination. Re-entered after each trader is added. The cloning workflow reaches the same page as 'Economic operators overview'.

evidence.note: click 'Add a consignor or exporter' — the empty state
click 'Same as consignee' — the populated state, after the consignor exists

pages.name: Search for an existing consignor or exporter (address book)
pages.conditional: Only when adding a consignor. The address-book search; carries the 'Create a new consignor or exporter' link. Every CHED-PP trace clicks straight through to create-new — the SEARCH ITSELF IS NEVER EXERCISED, so results/paging/no-results are uncovered.

evidence.note: the page's only action: click 'Create a new consignor or'

pages.name: Add a consignor or exporter (create new)
pages.conditional: Only after 'Create a new consignor or exporter'. Full address form: name, Address line 1/2/3 (2 and 3 optional), City or town, Postcode or ZIP code, Telephone number, Country, Email address.

evidence.note: fill 'City or town' — middle of the 62-71 range

pages.name: Create consignor or exporter confirmation
pages.conditional: Only after creating a consignor. A single 'Add to notification' button in every trace. Note the URL drops the /traders/ segment and keys on a trader UUID.

pages.name: Confirm billing details (Common User Charge)
pages.urlPattern: (no URL captured — the CUC trace has no URLs)
pages.conditional: CUC notifications only (isCuc). Sits between traders-addresses and review-notification. Hosts the #confirm-billing-address and #confirm-billing-address-email change links, and the final 'Save and continue'.

pages.name: Find a billing address (postcode lookup)
pages.conditional: CUC only, reached from the change-address link on confirm-billing-details. 'Postcode' textbox + 'Find address' button.

pages.name: Select the billing address
pages.conditional: CUC only, follows the postcode lookup. 'Select the address' combobox + 'Continue'.

pages.name: Change billing contact details
pages.conditional: CUC only, reached from the change-email link on confirm-billing-details. 'Email address' + 'Phone number' + 'Continue'.

pages.name: Review your notification (check your answers)
pages.conditional: always. Reached with :draftId pre-submission and :chedId post-submission. This page is TABBED post-submission and carries far more than a check-answers summary: a 'Checks' tab with an 'Accompanying documents' table (trace 83305f89 actions 145-148), the Valid/Rejected tabs after a split, the 'Split consignment' link, the delete route, and (amend mode) 'Review and submit'. Every create trace does a bare 'Save and continue' here, so the check-answers CONTENT is never asserted — see notes.

evidence.note: the page's only action in the create journey
post-split state: asserts the 'Valid' tab is visible — same URL, tabbed content
post-submission 'Checks' tab, asserting the 'Accompanying documents' table — the richest rendered state of this page in the corpus

pages.name: Declaration
pages.conditional: always — 'I/We have read and understood' checkbox + 'Submit notification'. Also the final step of the amend and copy-as-new journeys.

evidence.note: check 'I/We have read and understood'; action 77 submits and leaves

pages.name: Import notification sent (submission confirmation)
pages.conditional: always — terminal page of the create journey. Carries #reference-number (the CHEDPP.GB.YYYY.NNNNNNN) and a 'Return to dashboard' link. NOTE the URL flips from :draftId to :chedId here.

pages.name: Search for an existing consignee
pages.conditional: Spoke off traders-addresses via the 'Add a consignee' link. NEVER EXERCISED in the CHED-PP corpus — every trace uses 'Same as consignee' / #populate-importer instead. Known only from TradersAddressesPage.linkAddConsignee + SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts. Uncovered surface — a finding.

pages.name: Add a consignee (create new)
pages.conditional: Spoke off traders-addresses. Never exercised in the CHED-PP corpus; known only from ConsigneeCreationPage.ts. Uncovered surface — a finding.

pages.name: Consignee confirmation
pages.conditional: Spoke off traders-addresses. Never exercised in the CHED-PP corpus; known only from ConsigneeConfirmationPage.ts. Uncovered surface — a finding.

pages.name: Split consignment — confirm split
pages.conditional: Post-submission only, and only once an inspector has recorded mixed compliant/non-compliant check outcomes. Reached from the 'Split consignment' link on review-notification; a single 'Yes, split this consignment' button. Returns to review-notification with Valid/Rejected tabs and generates child CHEDs (…V / …R).

evidence.note: click 'Yes, split this consignment' — the page's only action

pages.name: Confirm you want to delete this notification
pages.conditional: Post-submission only. Reached from the dashboard/review of a submitted notification. Single confirm action.

pages.name: Clone a health or phytosanitary certificate — certificate type
pages.conditional: Cloning entry journey (an alternative to create-from-scratch), reached from the dashboard. Same 'Plants, plant products and other objects' radio as import-type but a distinct URL and heading.

pages.name: Search for a certificate to clone (certificate details)
pages.conditional: Cloning journey. Country + certificate reference + date of issue + party name, then Search.

pages.name: Cloning summary / 'You cannot clone this certificate'
pages.conditional: Cloning journey. ALL THREE cloning traces (ebd5a5d2, 470cd3de, 7d0bf2f6) render the 'You cannot clone this certificate' ERROR state here — the 'Clone' button never appears and the test times out. The successful clone path (Clone -> review -> transport -> GMS -> contacts -> economic operators -> commodity -> declaration, per submitClonedChedPPNotification) is therefore ENTIRELY UNCOVERED. Snapshot this to capture the error copy.

evidence.note: the timed-out wait for the 'Clone' button — the snapshot shows the error page

pages.name: How do you want to sign in? (SCP / Defra ID)
pages.urlPattern: (external identity provider — no in-app URL captured)
pages.conditional: Auth journey, before the dashboard. Only surfaced in trace 9a918b (B2C agent); every other trace reuses a stored storageState and never renders it. Out of scope for the CHED-PP rebuild but recorded for completeness.

pages.name: Assess consignments — BIP notifications search (inspector)
pages.conditional: SEPARATE APP (the BIP inspector decision service, on a different host). Not part of the CHED-PP create journey — recorded because 75 trace blocks land here. 'Keywords or CHED number' search + result links.

pages.name: Decision hub for a CHED (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Carries 'Record PHSI checks', 'override the risk decision', 'View notification overview', 'Request amendment', and #hmi-status (which auto-completes when the risk decision is 'no inspection').

pages.name: Record PHSI checks — select check types (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Documentary / Physical / Identity checkboxes; re-entered once per check type. Physical and Identity are only offered for commodities that require them.

pages.name: Record PHSI checks — multi-commodity (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Per-commodity checkboxes plus status (Compliant / Hold / Non compliant) and a reason. The query string selects which check type is being recorded.

pages.name: Checks recorded confirmation (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Links: 'Submit more checks', 'Return to decision hub', 'Return to work order'. 'Submit more checks' is absent when the commodity requires no further check types.

pages.name: Notification overview (inspector view)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Reached from 'View notification overview' on the decision hub; the step before 'Request amendment'.

pages.name: CHED overview (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Landed on after 'Return to Decision hub' in the rejection/document-access flow. Distinct from decision-notification-overview (different URL); only trace 83305f89 covers it. WARNING: NO action ever fires on this page — action 137 is the click ON the PHSI-confirmation page that lands here, and the trace jumps straight to a navigate away (139). The page's existence is evidenced only by the post-137 URL.

evidence.note: SNAPSHOT THE 'AFTER' STATE, NOT THE 'BEFORE'. Action 137 is 'Return to Decision hub' clicked on phsi-checks-confirmation; only its after-snapshot shows CHED-overview. A before-snapshot here yields the wrong page.

pages.name: Request amendment (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Amendment-reason textbox + 'Request amendment' button. Pushes the notification back to the notifier, who re-enters at notification-hub in amend mode.

pages.name: Accompanying documents / attachments tab (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app. Mirrors the notifier's accompanying-documents page (Document type / reference / date / 'Add attachment') but writes to #inspector-documents-table. Remains reachable after rejection (trace 83305f89).

pages.name: Upload attachment — decision context (upload service)
pages.conditional: Inspector-app hand-off to the same separate /upload/ service the notifier uses, but with a /decision/ context segment and a :chedId rather than a :draftId. Error states (invalid ZIP, empty file) render here — traces b5379598, bff12637.

pages.name: Do you want to override this risk decision? (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app, CHED-PP specific (note the -chedpp URL suffix). Reached from the 'override the risk decision' link on the decision hub. Only trace 60687c60 covers it.

pages.name: Why are you overriding this consignment risk decision? (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app, CHED-PP specific. Reason radios (e.g. 'No inspector available') + 'Confirm override'.

pages.name: This consignment no longer requires inspection (inspector)
pages.conditional: Inspector app, CHED-PP specific. Terminal confirmation of the override.

notes:
SCOPE: 59 journey files were present (the brief said 54). 34 pages sit on the linear create journey (order 0-33); 22 more are off-path (traders spokes, cloning, delete, split, auth, and the separate BIP inspector app).

TOP DEDUP RULING — commodity-class is NOT a page. Trace e10b7cd5 action 26 fires getByLabel('Select class') while the URL is /commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species. CommodityClassPage.ts models a dropdown on the variety page. Any inventory listing commodity-class and variety-of-genus-and-species as two pages is double-counting.

URL FIDELITY WARNING for the snapshot wave: the pageGuess URLs are LLM-extracted and the two richest traces (1f5b6eef, 219b882e) LAG BY ONE PAGE across the commodity region — 219b882e puts 'Internal market' on page-4 when the workflow, the a11y spec, e10b7cd5 and the CSV trace all put it on page-5. Commodity-region evidence is therefore taken from e10b7cd5 (clean URLs), NOT from the higher-action-count traces. The one boundary still unsettled is page-3 vs page-4: the workflow's comments put btnAddSpecies on page-4 (Commodity Basic Description) while 1f5b6eef/219b882e attribute the 'Add' click to the page-3 URL. Evidence for both pages was chosen to be safe under either reading.

UNCOVERED SURFACE (findings, not absences of requirement):
1. gms-declaration — no CHED-PP spec sets gmsDeclaration:'Yes'. Zero traces. Known only from ched-pp-workflows.ts + GMSDeclarationPage.ts. Empty evidence.
2. The consignee sub-journey (consignee-search / consignee-create / consignee-confirmation) — reachable via 'Add a consignee' on traders-addresses, but every trace uses 'Same as consignee' / #populate-importer. Empty evidence.
3. The successful CLONE path — all three cloning traces (ebd5a5d2, 470cd3de, 7d0bf2f6) hit 'You cannot clone this certificate' at cloning/summary and time out. Everything submitClonedChedPPNotification does after the Clone button (review -> transport -> GMS -> contacts -> economic operators -> commodity -> declaration) is unexercised.
4. origin-of-import's three questions ('Does this consignment conform...', 'Does your consignment require a health certificate?', 'Will the consignment change after BCP...') — every trace accepts the defaults and clicks through, so the page renders but its fields are never touched.
5. The consignor address-book SEARCH — consignor-search is only ever used as a pass-through to 'Create a new'. Results, paging and no-results states are uncovered.
6. review-notification's check-answers CONTENT — every create trace does a bare 'Save and continue'. The summary rows the new service must render are not evidenced by any assertion.
7. contact-details and nominated-contact — clicked straight through in every trace; field surface unverified.
8. decision-ched-overview — the URL is reached but NO action ever fires on it, so only an 'after' snapshot of trace 83305f89 action 137 can capture it.

WATCH OUT — review-notification is heavier than its name suggests. Post-submission it is a TABBED page (Checks tab with an 'Accompanying documents' table; Valid/Rejected tabs after a split) served under the SAME URL and heading as the pre-submission check-your-answers page. Treating it as one simple summary page would under-specify it badly; treating each tab as its own page would double-count. It is one page with several states.

ARCHITECTURAL SIGNALS worth carrying into the rebuild: (a) attachments are handled by a SEPARATE /upload/ app on both the notifier and inspector sides, keyed /upload/:id/0/{notification|decision}/attach; (b) the id flips from DRAFT.GB.YYYY.NNNNNNN to CHEDPP.GB.YYYY.NNNNNNN at submission, and the same page (review, overview) is served under both; (c) the URL scheme is incoherent legacy — positional page-1..page-5 names sit alongside semantic ones (/commodity/input-method, /transport/before-bip), and the same logical step appears under /consignment/details and /commodity/details in different traces. The rebuild should not inherit any of this.

SECRETS: no credentials were copied into this file. Trace e10b7cd5 action 105 contains an OAuth token POST — already redacted at extraction time.
